subject: Anthropology
title: Code-Switching
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Code-Switching.

hook.text: She speaks English at work, Spanish at home, and mixes both with friends. Three settings, three language modes.

buildup.text: Code-switching is shifting between languages or dialects within a conversation based on social context.

discovery.text: It's not random or confused. Speakers switch strategically to signal identity, solidarity, authority, or intimacy.

twist.text: Code-switching was once stigmatized as broken language. Linguists now recognize it as a sign of high linguistic skill.

climax.text: Code-switching reveals that speakers are social actors — adjusting their language to navigate complex social worlds.

punchline.text: Switching codes means reading the room.

quiz.question: Why do people code-switch?
options: Because they can't speak either language well
To signal identity and navigate social contexts
